behavior_summary: Provides gameplay behaviour for a marble item: describing itself when examined, allowing being thrown by a player/process, and responding to collisions/hits by being launched.
MARBLE::look displays a short description string ("rolling sphere ") to the examiner and then returns per the U8P_MARBLE::look-event convention.

MARBLE::use computes a throw vector from the marble to a supplied target (using Item::getCX/getCY), normalizes axes into small steps (-1/0/1), injects randomness (urandom) as jitter and sign variation, and calls Item::hurl to launch the marble.
MARBLE::hit marks the item with a status bit (OR 0x4000) when it is the source of a hit and, if the item type equals the marble type (0x00D7), clears another status bit (AND with bit_not 0x0400).

MARBLE::gotHit converts an incoming strike/impulse into movement: if magnitude > 0 it computes scaled displacement/velocity components from striker vs marble positions, calls Item::hurl to apply motion, and for MARBLE-type items may invoke a second hurl with adjusted params.
notable_control_flow: Event-driven: main entry is a 'U8P_MARBLE::look' event (MARBLE::look). Other behaviours are implemented as helpers invoked on use, hit, and gotHit events.
MARBLE::use contains normalization branches that map raw deltas to discrete direction steps and branches applying random jitter before the final hurl call.
MARBLE::hit unconditionally sets a status bit then conditionally clears another bit only when Item::getType == 0x00D7 (marble-specific branch).

MARBLE::gotHit early-exits if incoming magnitude/strike is zero; otherwise it computes and scales components, conditionally performs hurl, and has a type-specific duplicate-hurl branch for type 0x00D7.
open_questions: Exact semantics and field ordering of Item::hurl parameters (what each numeric arg represents: x/y velocity, z, flags/pid) — inferred but not canonical.
Precise meaning of the event argument slots passed into use/hit/gotHit (BP+0Ah/BP+0Ch) — inferred as target coords or strike/magnitude but not firmly documented.
Why MARBLE-type (0x00D7) triggers a secondary hurl in gotHit — likely a special-case behaviour but exact gameplay intent is not explicit.
Whether additional global flags or world state are affected by these routines beyond the per-item status bits shown (no globals read/written in disassembly).

main_function.name: U8P_MARBLE::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the item's 'U8P_MARBLE::look' (examine) event by displaying a short description string to the examiner and then suspending/returning according to the U8P_MARBLE::look-event convention.
entry_conditions: Event 0 — triggered when the Avatar or a process examines/looks at the marble item (examine/inspect action).

function_map.name: U8P_MARBLE::look
function_map.role: Entry point for the item's examine/U8P_MARBLE::look event; calls Item::bark with the description string and returns.

function_map.name: U8P_MARBLE::use
function_map.role: Handle a player/process 'use' action to throw the marble: compute deltas to a target, normalize directions, add random jitter, and call Item::hurl.

function_map.name: U8P_MARBLE::hit
function_map.role: Handle the marble being the source of a hit: set status bits and clear a bit for MARBLE-type items.

function_map.name: U8P_MARBLE::gotHit
function_map.role: Handle the marble receiving an external impulse: compute push vector from striker, scale by magnitude, and call Item::hurl; has a MARBLE-type special-case.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: External UI/display call used to show the item's description/message to a process/player.

function_map.name: Item::hurl
function_map.role: Engine function to launch/move the item with supplied numeric components/flags.

function_map.name: Item::getCX
function_map.role: Return the item's X coordinate used for delta/trajectory computation.

function_map.name: Item::getCY
function_map.role: Return the item's Y coordinate used for delta/trajectory computation.

function_map.name: urandom
function_map.role: Provide random values for jitter and sign/randomization in throw computations.

function_map.name: Item::orStatus
function_map.role: Set a status bit(s) on the item (used in hit to set 0x4000).

function_map.name: Item::andStatus
function_map.role: Apply AND to item status (used to clear specific bits by AND with bit_not mask).

function_map.name: Item::getType
function_map.role: Return the item's type id; code compares to 0x00D7 to detect marble-specific behaviour.

internal_calls.purpose: Display the short description string 'rolling sphere ' to the examining process.
Read marble X coordinate to compute delta to target for throw.
Read marble Y coordinate to compute delta to target for throw.
Add randomized jitter and occasional sign/step flipping to the computed throw vector.
Launch the marble using the computed/normalized/randomized components and context pid.
Set the item's status bit 0x4000 when the item is a hit source.
Check whether the item type equals 0x00D7 so the code can apply a marble-specific status clear.
Clear status bit 0x0400 for MARBLE-type items by ANDing with bit_not mask.
Read marble X coordinate to compute push vector from striker.
Read marble Y coordinate to compute push vector from striker.
Apply computed impulse/movement to the marble; may be called twice for MARBLE-type items with adjusted parameters.
Detect marble type (0x00D7) to decide on the secondary hurl special-case.

external_calls.purpose: UI bark to show the description string to the viewer.
Get X coordinate of item for throw calculations.
Get Y coordinate of item for throw calculations.
Introduce randomness into throw direction and magnitude.
Perform the in-world throw/move of the marble.
Clear status flags conditionally when the item is a marble type.
Identify item type to apply marble-specific logic.
Apply physics-like impulse: move the marble in response to hits.
Check for marble type to possibly perform a second hurl.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides gameplay behaviour for a marble item: describing itself when examined, allowing being thrown by a player/process, and responding to collisions/hits by being launched.
